2606.19616.title: Before the Pull Request: Mining Multi-Agent Coordination
2606.19616.paper_summary: Autonomous coding agents now open millions of pull requests, yet large-scale studies find their PRs are produced faster but accepted less often - a coordination and trust gap that pull-request-level telemetry cannot explain. We argue the missing signal lives before the PR, in how concurrent agents claim, divide, and collide over shared work. We study this process through grite, our open-source coordination substrate that needs no central server and stores its records inside git itself, so its append-only, signed event log captures the coordination process directly. We show that (i) this shared substrate reduces duplicate and conflicting work at bounded overhead - the share of work that merely re-does a teammate's task falls from 78% to 0% while useful throughput more than triples; (ii) every agent's copy of the log converges to the same state with no write silently dropped, where a file-based tracker loses concurrent writes; and (iii) the log is a mineable artefact from which concrete failure modes - conflicting edits, lock starvation, redundant rediscovery, race-to-close - are automatically recoverable with provenance, several invisible in pull-request history. We release the dataset, harness, and mining toolkit.
2606.19616.paper_summary_zh: 自主編碼代理現在開啟了數百萬個拉取請求，但大規模研究發現它們的 PR 產生速度更快，但被接受的頻率卻較低——這是一個協調和信任的差距，無法用拉取請求層級的遙測來解釋。我們認為缺失的信號存在於 PR 之前，體現在並行代理如何聲明、劃分和碰撞共享工作。我們通過 grite 研究這一過程，這是一個開源的協調基底，無需中央伺服器，並將其記錄存儲在 git 本身內，因此其僅附加的、簽名的事件日誌直接捕捉協調過程。我們顯示 (i) 這個共享基底在有限的開銷下減少了重複和衝突的工作——僅僅重做隊友任務的工作比例從 78% 降至 0%，而有用的產出則增長了三倍以上；(ii) 每個代理的日誌副本在沒有靜默丟失寫入的情況下收斂到相同的狀態，而基於文件的追蹤器會丟失並發寫入；(iii) 日誌是一個可挖掘的文物，從中可以自動恢復具體的失敗模式——衝突編輯、鎖飢餓、冗餘重新發現、競爭關閉——並附帶來源，其中幾個在拉取請求歷史中是不可見的。我們釋放了數據集、工具和挖掘工具包。
2606.19616.author: Dipankar Sarkar et.al.
